mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening corporate output capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth rates coincide with negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% force expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases or Rank IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE trends yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals abrupt supply chain contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth months yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.
